column6: description
File missing - migration DB says ported but file does not exist. Original ArrayUtils.java has: contained, append, firstOf, indexOf, lastOf, lastIndexOf, toArray.

Compound unit algebra gaps in SassNumber: dart-sass lib/src/value/number.dart + number/{complex,single_unit,unitless}.dart implements three specialized subclasses (UnitlessSassNumber, SingleUnitSassNumber, ComplexSassNumber) with full numerator/denominator normalization, cross-type coercion (length*time, freq/angle), and conversion-factor caching. ssg-sass uses a single flat SassNumber with best-effort unit handling. Needs: multi-unit product/quotient canonicalization, compatible-unit detection across complex numerators, and coerceValueToUnit fallback paths. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

fuzzyEquals / fuzzyHashCode: dart-sass lib/src/util/number.dart defines an epsilon-aware comparison (fuzzyEquals, fuzzyLessThan, fuzzyRound, fuzzyAsInt, fuzzyCheckRange) used throughout number arithmetic and the extend matcher. ssg-sass appears to compare doubles directly in several paths, causing false inequality near 1e-11 rounding errors. Port the full fuzzy-number module.

Number precision and SassScript serialization: dart-sass SassNumber.toString / util/number.dart uses an exactly-10-decimal-digit precision (_epsilon = 1e-11) and printNumber that trims trailing zeros, handles negative zero, and emits 'Infinity' as unquoted strings. ssg-sass likely diverges at extreme magnitudes and for -0.

Calculation simplification: dart-sass lib/src/value/calculation.dart implements round/mod/rem/abs/sign/sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2/sqrt/exp/pow/log/hypot/clamp as first-class calc-level operations that can round-trip CSS-math-3 expressions, collapse only when all operands are known numbers, and preserve special keywords (infinity, NaN, pi, e). ssg-sass SassCalculation only covers calc/min/max/clamp; the other CSS Math functions fall through to string-based paths. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Calculation operator simplification: dart-sass lib/src/value/calculation.dart operate()/simplify() collapses a+b, a-b, a*b, a/b under unit compatibility, folds parenthesized subexpressions, and detects definitely-invalid-CSS (units + unitless mix, divide-by-zero) with precise SassScriptException. ssg-sass simplification is shallow and does not recurse into nested CalculationOperation trees. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Color-space conversions missing primaries round-trip: dart-sass lib/src/value/color/conversions.dart + space/*.dart implements full CIE lab<->xyz<->lms<->oklab<->oklch<->prophoto<->a98-rgb<->display-p3<->rec2020 matrices with D50/D65 adaptation (Bradford) and per-channel Bradford matrices. ssg-sass covers sRGB / HSL / lab / oklab / oklch with simplified matrices; non-sRGB predefined spaces may round-trip lossy. Verify matrices against dart-sass conversions.dart constants.

Gamut mapping (color.to-gamut): dart-sass lib/src/value/color/gamut_map_method/{clip,local_minde}.dart implements the CSS Color 4 gamut-mapping algorithm (MINDE-based LCH chroma reduction) and clip fallback. ssg-sass does not implement gamut mapping; out-of-gamut oklch/lab colors are emitted unclamped. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Powerless / missing channels: dart-sass lib/src/value/color.dart tracks per-channel 'missing' state (none keyword) and per-space 'powerless' channels (e.g. hue is powerless when saturation=0), propagating correctly through mix() and toSpace(). ssg-sass has partial none support; powerless analysis during interpolation is incomplete. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Color interpolation methods: dart-sass lib/src/value/color/interpolation_method.dart implements hue interpolation hints (shorter, longer, increasing, decreasing, specified) per CSS Color 4. ssg-sass color.mix honors space but likely not the hue-interpolation hint grammar.

sass:color module-4 API: dart-sass lib/src/functions/color.dart exposes color.channel, color.space, color.is-in-gamut, color.is-legacy, color.is-powerless, color.is-missing, color.to-space, color.to-gamut, color.same, color.adjust/change/scale with , -interpolation. ssg-sass implements legacy rgb/hsl/adjust/scale/change and mix but is missing the Color-4 introspection and to-gamut/to-space entry points. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Global color functions deprecation warnings: dart-sass lib/src/functions/color.dart wraps every global (red, green, blue, lighten, darken, mix, saturate, desaturate, adjust-hue, transparentize, opacify, fade-in, fade-out, ...) with withDeprecationWarning('color') or globalBuiltin deprecation. ssg-sass does not emit these deprecation warnings.

sass:math gaps: dart-sass lib/src/functions/math.dart implements div/percentage/clamp/hypot with full unit checks, math.exp, math.log with base, math.pow with fractional exponents, math.cbrt, math.div with compound units, math.ceil/floor/round with precision, math.sin/cos/tan/asin/acos/atan/atan2 with angle unit coercion. ssg-sass covers the common subset but is missing edge-case unit validation and atan2.

sass:meta gaps: dart-sass lib/src/functions/meta.dart provides meta.load-css with dynamic URL +  configuration that executes a new module at call-site, meta.accepts-content, meta.content-exists (requires mixin call stack), meta.calc-name, meta.calc-args, meta.get-function with $module, meta.apply. ssg-sass meta.load-css only handles literal strings and content-exists is a placeholder (tracked in SHORTCUTS). REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

sass:selector edge cases: dart-sass lib/src/functions/selector.dart uses the full SelectorList parser including bogus-combinator detection, placeholder extension, and returns unified selectors with real specificity metadata. ssg-sass selector functions work for common shapes; verify against sass-spec selector test cases for is-superselector and extend.

sass:string edge cases: dart-sass lib/src/functions/string.dart string.slice uses 1-based indices with negative support and Unicode code-point boundaries; string.length returns code-point count not UTF-16 units. Verify ssg-sass string functions handle surrogate pairs correctly on JVM/JS/Native.

sass:list list.slash and list.separator: dart-sass lib/src/functions/list.dart supports slash-separated lists as a distinct ListSeparator.slash; list.join with $separator auto/comma/space/slash, list.is-bracketed. Verify ssg-sass handles slash separators through all list operations.

Expression lexer text-based (already noted in SHORTCUTS): dart-sass lib/src/parse/stylesheet.dart _expression / _singleExpression is a proper token-aware descent that tracks whitespace-sensitive operator disambiguation, unary +/-, binary op precedence, parenthesized subexpressions, map literals, slash-separated lists, interpolation. ssg-sass uses a text-collector fallback that rarely mis-splits on unusual shapes.

Plain-CSS parser (CssParser): dart-sass lib/src/parse/css.dart is a distinct parser subclass that rejects Sass-only syntax (variables, @mixin, @include, nesting, interpolation in selectors, #{}). ssg-sass CssParser is a skeleton — plain CSS is parsed through ScssParser, so ssg-sass would silently accept Sass-only syntax in .css input. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Indented (sass) parser override hooks: SHORTCUTS notes SassParser throws UnsupportedOperationException on styleRuleSelector/expectStatementSeparator/atEndOfStatement/lookingAtChildren/scanElse/children/statements. Multi-line selector continuations and indent-sensitive edge cases are unsupported. dart-sass lib/src/parse/sass.dart is a full subclass override.

Media query level 4: dart-sass lib/src/parse/media_query.dart parses modern range syntax (screen and (400px <= width < 1000px)), 'only'/'not'/'and'/'or' with correct precedence, media-type-less queries. ssg-sass MediaQueryParser likely covers the old syntax only.

@supports modern selector() and font-format()/font-tech(): dart-sass supports the 2022 CR additions (selector(...), font-format(opentype), font-tech(color-COLRv1)). ssg-sass covers selector() per SHORTCUTS; font-format/font-tech untracked.

Nested variable declaration / !default with maps: dart-sass lib/src/parse/stylesheet.dart variableDeclaration handles declaration inside declaration block, namespace-qualified variable writes (module.$var: value), and map-literal as default value. Verify ssg-sass handles all three.
@else after expression newline: dart-sass stylesheet.dart _scanElse tolerates loud comments between @if block and @else. Text-based fallback may break on comment-separated else clauses.
@charset handling: dart-sass consumes @charset and emits a UTF-8 BOM in compressed mode when non-ASCII is present. Verify ssg-sass serializer writes the BOM and suppresses @charset in output.

Nested declarations / mixed-decls: dart-sass lib/src/visitor/evaluate.dart enforces the mixed-decls deprecation (declarations cannot appear after nested rules in the same parent) and the ordering rules for interleaved declarations. ssg-sass may permit any interleaving without warning. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

@at-root queries (#{...}, (with: media), (without: all)): dart-sass evaluate.dart visitAtRootRule respects 'with'/'without' lists including pseudo-at-rules 'rule' and 'all', bubbling the selector/media tree up or down accordingly. ssg-sass handles basic @at-root; verify (with/without) query parser handles the full token set. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Unitless-to-unit arithmetic division deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart _addExceptionSpan and slashDiv deprecation track every '/' that is used for division in a context that is ambiguous with plain-CSS slash (font: 10px/12px ...), emitting the 'slash-div' deprecation with code fixups. ssg-sass does not emit slash-div warnings. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Strict unary operator deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart detects ambiguous +/- where whitespace asymmetry suggests the user meant list-separator vs binary op and emits 'strict-unary' deprecation. Not implemented in ssg-sass.

Function units deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart emits 'function-units' when built-in functions receive invalid-unit arguments (e.g. math.sin(2px)). ssg-sass likely errors or silently succeeds without the deprecation framing.
Global-builtin deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart wraps global red/blue/lighten/etc. with 'global-builtin' deprecation pointing users at sass:color. Not surfaced in ssg-sass.

@import deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart emits the 'import' deprecation for every @import of a Sass file (the whole @import feature is deprecated in favor of @use/@forward). Not emitted by ssg-sass.

Module configuration with private variables: dart-sass evaluate.dart rejects configuring a private ($_foo) variable in @use/@forward 'with' and emits 'with-private'. Verify ssg-sass behavior. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Nested @use / dynamic module chains: dart-sass evaluate.dart registers each used module with its source-span and evaluates transitive @forward chains with shadow views (module/shadowed_view.dart, module/forwarded_view.dart). ssg-sass module tree works for common cases; verify deep forward chains and the 'as *' re-export semantics. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Parent selector in content blocks: dart-sass evaluate.dart visitIncludeRule captures the ambient & when passing ContentBlock so '&' inside the block refers to the caller's selector, not the mixin's. Verify ssg-sass captures ambient selector correctly across @content boundaries.

Extend second-law merge matrix: SHORTCUTS notes the 'second law of extend' _mergeTrailingCombinators matrix in dart-sass extend/functions.dart is skipped. This affects compound extends where the targets share trailing sibling/adjacent-sibling combinators (+, ~, >): ssg-sass falls back to plain concatenation, producing slightly different but valid selectors. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Extend weave sibling-combinator interleaving: SHORTCUTS notes mid-complex sibling combinator interleavings fall back to plain concatenation. dart-sass extend/functions.dart weave handles all combinator pairs through a case table; port the missing combinations. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Merged extensions: dart-sass extend/merged_extension.dart tracks the lattice of extensions that stack via @extend chains so their sources can be reported in error/warn messages. ssg-sass does not track a merged-extension lattice, which limits error provenance.

Bogus combinator deprecation: dart-sass extend/extension_store.dart emits 'bogus-combinators' deprecation when a selector has leading/trailing/repeated combinators (like '+ foo', 'foo >') during @extend resolution. ssg-sass does not warn.

PseudoSelector specializations: dart-sass ast/selector/pseudo.dart encodes per-pseudo-name logic for :is, :where, :not, :matches, :has, :host, :host-context, :slotted with custom unify/extend/specificity rules (e.g. :where has 0 specificity, :is takes max of inner). ssg-sass likely treats all selector-argument pseudos uniformly. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Selector specificity computation: dart-sass ast/selector/*.dart computes specificity as a (a,b,c) triple per CSS Selectors 4 with :is/:where/:not handling, then exposes minSpecificity/maxSpecificity for extend-time checks. Verify ssg-sass specificity matches.

Complex-component leading combinators: dart-sass ast/selector/complex_component.dart tracks prefix combinators on each component (for nesting). ssg-sass may flatten combinators into textual joins, losing fidelity for '& + .b' inside nested rules.

Attribute selector namespace + modifiers: dart-sass ast/selector/attribute.dart supports namespaced attributes (|attr, ns|attr, *|attr) and the case-sensitivity modifier (i, s). Verify ssg-sass parses all three.

Serializer source-map per-token mappings: SHORTCUTS notes ssg-sass emits one mapping per style rule and declaration. dart-sass visitor/serialize.dart emits per-token mappings (property name, colon, each space-separated value, semicolon) via source_map_buffer.dart and util/source_map_buffer.dart. Port per-token granularity and sourcesContent.

Compressed mode edge cases: dart-sass serialize.dart _writeOptionalSpace / _writeLineFeed omit whitespace aggressively (no space after ':', strip trailing ';', inline @media/@supports contents). Verify ssg-sass compressed output matches byte-for-byte on sass-spec compressed suite.

Quoted-string serialization: dart-sass serialize.dart _visitQuotedString uses double quotes by default, switches to single if the string contains a double, and escapes non-printable / surrogates per CSS. Verify ssg-sass escape-rule matches (\0-\1f, quote flipping, hex-escape termination).

URL serialization: dart-sass serialize.dart url() contents are written without further escaping when already quoted; unquoted url(data:...) preserved verbatim. Verify ssg-sass does not double-escape url() payloads.

Custom property value preservation: dart-sass serialize.dart visitDeclaration special-cases custom properties (--foo: bar): emits the raw declaration value verbatim, preserving newlines and comments inside the value, only evaluating #{} interpolation. Verify ssg-sass matches including multi-line custom properties.

Loud comment interpolation and placement: dart-sass serialize.dart preserves /* loud */ comments at their source location and evaluates #{} in them. Verify ssg-sass places loud comments identically between nested rules.
Unicode range serialization: dart-sass serialize.dart emits U+0-10FFFF forms and collapses ranges. Unicode-range values are a minor but visible divergence on font suites.

Importer canonicalize-context: dart-sass lib/src/importer/canonicalize_context.dart exposes containingUrl and canonical-url chain to custom importers, required by the modern JS importer API. ssg-sass importers do not expose this context, limiting importer authoring fidelity.

Load-path precedence: dart-sass lib/src/importer/filesystem.dart walks loadPaths in order, resolving partials (_foo.scss), extension variants (.scss -> .sass -> .css), and _index variants at each level. Verify ssg-sass FilesystemImporter enforces the exact resolution order and ambiguity errors (two matches in same dir).

fs-importer-cwd deprecation: dart-sass emits the 'fs-importer-cwd' deprecation when the filesystem importer resolves a file relative to cwd rather than the entry-point's containing directory. Not implemented.

NodePackageImporter exports field: dart-sass lib/src/importer/node_package.dart resolves package subpath exports per the Node resolution spec, including conditional exports (sass, style, default), nested subpath patterns, and import-maps. ssg-sass covers the main/style/sass top-level fields; conditional/subpath exports need verification.

Import cycle error span: dart-sass lib/src/import_cache.dart throws SassException with the full cycle chain. Verify ssg-sass cycle error lists the offending chain rather than only the two cycle-endpoints.
relative-canonical deprecation: dart-sass emits 'relative-canonical' deprecation when an importer returns a relative URL from canonicalize. ssg-sass does not track or warn.

Deprecation framework: dart-sass lib/src/deprecation.dart defines an enum of 30+ deprecations (call-string, elseif, moz-document, relative-canonical, new-global, color-module-compat, slash-div, bogus-combinators, strict-unary, function-units, duplicate-var-flags, null-alpha, abs-percent, fs-importer-cwd, css-function-mixin, mixed-decls, feature-exists, color-4-api, color-functions, legacy-js-api, import, global-builtin, type-function, compile-string-relative-url, misplaced-rest, with-private, if-function, function-name) with deprecatedIn/obsoleteIn version ranges and DeprecationProcessingLogger (lib/src/logger/deprecation_processing.dart) that enforces --silence-deprecation / --fatal-deprecation / --future-deprecation flags. ssg-sass has no deprecation framework at all — none of these warnings are emitted. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Logger chain (default/stderr/tracking/deprecation_processing/js_to_dart): dart-sass lib/src/logger/*.dart defines a composable Logger interface with warn/debug hooks, source-span formatting, and ANSI colorization. ssg-sass collects warnings on CompileResult but does not offer pluggable loggers. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Future-deprecation activation: dart-sass supports opt-in to future deprecations via a silence/fatal/future flag set. With no deprecation framework, ssg-sass cannot surface future-deprecation warnings for early migration.

Error span fidelity (synthetic placeholders): SHORTCUTS notes selector/media/at-root sub-parsers build FileSpan.synthetic placeholders with synthesized file URLs. dart-sass uses lib/src/util/lazy_file_span.dart and multi_span.dart to attach real spans through every error path. Many ssg-sass errors point at placeholder locations.
MultiSpanException: dart-sass exception.dart defines MultiSpanSassException that attaches secondary spans (e.g. variable was defined here + used here). ssg-sass has only single-span exceptions.
Stack trace / call chain: dart-sass SassException toString formats a multi-line trace 'from line X of file Y' walking the include stack. Verify ssg-sass prints the full include/use chain on error.

Module system views: dart-sass lib/src/module/{built_in,forwarded_view,shadowed_view}.dart provides lazy member maps that honor show/hide/as-prefix filters at lookup time rather than at load time. This keeps configuration propagation O(1) per lookup and avoids copying module state. ssg-sass may eagerly materialize the merged view, which is correct but slower and can mask some re-export edge cases. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

Configuration propagation order: dart-sass lib/src/configuration.dart + configured_value.dart track which configured values have been used, and dart-sass raises an error when an @use supplies a value that the target module does not accept. Verify ssg-sass enforces 'unused configuration' errors.

Variable scoping inside nested imports: dart-sass lib/src/environment.dart distinguishes the @import semantic (declarations leak into calling scope) from @use (isolated module). ssg-sass must match the scoping for code still on the @import path.

@forward show/hide mixins vs functions: dart-sass evaluate.dart visitForwardRule applies show/hide to the union of variables, functions, and mixins with a single pass but produces a distinct ForwardedView per member kind. Verify ssg-sass does not cross-leak mixin names into function namespace or vice versa. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

StylesheetGraph change detection: dart-sass lib/src/stylesheet_graph.dart tracks modification times and import edges for watch-mode incremental rebuild. ssg-sass has a minimal graph (no watch support). Needed if ssg-sass is ever run with a watcher.

Keyword-args tracking: dart-sass lib/src/value/argument_list.dart tracks which keyword arguments were consumed to raise 'unknown keyword argument' errors exactly once per unused keyword. Verify ssg-sass behavior matches.

SassMap equality and ordering: dart-sass lib/src/value/map.dart preserves insertion order and compares by key equality, treating an empty map as equal to an empty list (for Sass compat). Verify ssg-sass.
Quoted string identity: dart-sass lib/src/value/string.dart distinguishes quoted vs unquoted and makes unquoted strings never equal to quoted with the same text. This affects map keys.

First-class SassMixin: dart-sass lib/src/value/mixin.dart implements SassMixin as a first-class value returned by meta.get-mixin, accepted by meta.apply. ssg-sass SHORTCUTS notes SassMixin exists but verify meta.apply is wired.

FindDependenciesVisitor dynamic load-css: SHORTCUTS notes handles literal strings only. dart-sass visitor/find_dependencies.dart recurses into interpolation and dynamic meta.load-css arguments to build a conservative dependency set for incremental builds.

IsPlainCssVisitor / ExpressionToCalc / AnySelector / EveryCss / IsCalculationSafe: dart-sass visitor/*.dart has seven auxiliary visitors for AST classification used by simplification and serialization. ssg-sass implements a subset; verify each has a working counterpart or document as N/A.
duplicate-var-flags deprecation: dart-sass stylesheet.dart rejects !default !default or !global !global on one variable. Deprecation warning not emitted in ssg-sass.
elseif deprecation: dart-sass stylesheet.dart accepts legacy @elseif spelling and warns via the 'elseif' deprecation. ssg-sass either errors or silently accepts.
moz-document deprecation: dart-sass stylesheet.dart emits 'moz-document' for @-moz-document rules (only empty url-prefix() allowed). Not warned by ssg-sass.
css-function-mixin deprecation: dart-sass stylesheet.dart forbids @function/@mixin names that begin with '--'. Not enforced by ssg-sass.
misplaced-rest deprecation: dart-sass stylesheet.dart detects a rest parameter that appears before a positional or named parameter and warns via 'misplaced-rest'. Not detected by ssg-sass.
function-name deprecation: dart-sass stylesheet.dart rejects uppercase reserved function names (URL, CALC, etc.) as user function names via 'function-name'. Not enforced in ssg-sass.
type-function deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart warns on user-defined functions named 'type' (colliding with meta.type-of future). Not warned in ssg-sass.
new-global deprecation: dart-sass evaluate.dart warns when !global is used to declare a new variable (rather than mutate an existing one). Not warned in ssg-sass.
abs-percent deprecation: dart-sass functions/color.dart warns when the Sass abs() function receives a percent argument (ambiguous with CSS abs()). Not emitted in ssg-sass.
call-string deprecation: dart-sass functions/meta.dart warns when meta.call() receives a bare string rather than a first-class function. Not emitted in ssg-sass.
feature-exists deprecation: dart-sass functions/meta.dart deprecates meta.feature-exists entirely (all features now assumed present). Not warned in ssg-sass.
if-function deprecation: dart-sass deprecates the global Sass if($condition, $if-true, $if-false) in favor of meta.if (or @if). Not warned in ssg-sass.
null-alpha deprecation: dart-sass functions/color.dart warns when rgb/hsl receive null as alpha (instead of treating as 'no alpha specified'). Not detected in ssg-sass.

color-4-api deprecation: dart-sass functions/color.dart warns on legacy-API uses of sass:color that are incompatible with Color-4 (passing rgb values outside 0-255 expecting clamp, etc.). Not warned in ssg-sass.

color-module-compat deprecation: dart-sass functions/color.dart warns when color.* functions are called with legacy-API conventions that don't match the Color-4 semantics (color-module-compat). Not warned in ssg-sass.
Interpolation in at-rule names: dart-sass stylesheet.dart supports @#{foo}-rule with interpolation in the at-rule name. Verify ssg-sass.
Placeholder selectors in @forward: dart-sass stylesheet.dart allows %foo in @forward show/hide lists. Verify ssg-sass selector-filter syntax covers placeholders.

Nested rules inside declarations (sub-property syntax): dart-sass stylesheet.dart permits 'font: { family: sans; size: 1em; }' that expands to font-family/font-size. Verify ssg-sass parses the declaration-with-nested-children form.

First-class calc with interpolation: dart-sass evaluate.dart permits #{} inside calc() and feeds the result back into the calculation simplifier if it parses as a number. ssg-sass likely treats interpolated calc contents as opaque strings.

Plain CSS function preservation: dart-sass evaluate.dart distinguishes a Sass function call from a plain-CSS function token (unknown-function) and emits the latter verbatim. ssg-sass must preserve unknown function calls like env(), var(), url() untouched. REOPEN: marked resolved by automated scan but underlying code uses stubs/simplifications. Requires full faithful port.

CSS variable (var()) fallback: dart-sass evaluate.dart treats var(--foo, default) as a special unquoted string that never participates in arithmetic. Verify ssg-sass does not try to evaluate the fallback as a Sass expression.

Evaluator statement-search / source-interpolation: dart-sass visitor/statement_search.dart and source_interpolation.dart support reverse lookups used by language server and find-all-references. Not ported — acceptable for compiler-only, but blocks future IDE integration.

Watcher / repl / executable: dart-sass lib/src/executable/*.dart and util/multi_dir_watcher.dart implement watch mode, REPL, and CLI options. Explicitly out of scope for ssg-sass (library only), but should be noted so nobody assumes feature parity.

Syntax auto-detection: dart-sass lib/src/syntax.dart picks SCSS/SASS/CSS from file extension with an explicit override. Verify ssg-sass Compiler applies the same mapping and honors .css -> CssParser strict mode (currently relevant only once ISS-019 lands).

Legacy color accessors on non-legacy colors: dart-sass color.dart throws 'can't get red channel from non-legacy color' when red/green/blue/hue/saturation/lightness is called on lab/oklch. Verify ssg-sass emits the same error (not silent conversion).
Color equality legacy vs modern: dart-sass color.dart treats lab(50% 0 0) and a legacy-rgb equivalent as unequal even if they round-trip. Verify ssg-sass equality respects the space boundary.

Color serialization per space: dart-sass serialize.dart emits lab(…)/oklch(…)/color(display-p3 …) forms with correct channel clamping rules (percent vs number) and 'none' keyword round-trip. Verify ssg-sass matches on the full ColorSpacesSuite sass-spec cases.
— Partially fixed in commit 2f4a7a8 (B001, +37 cases). Remaining work tracked as ISS-235. REOPEN: prior fix was an ad-hoc patch. Requires full faithful port of the original dart-sass method(s).

async/sync evaluator split: dart-sass ships both async_evaluate.dart and evaluate.dart generated from a single source via tool/grind/synchronize.dart. ssg-sass is sync-only, which is correct but means the async-importer JS API is unsupported.

B001: Escape sequence decoding -- partial fix landed (+37), ~127 remaining cases need _parseInterpolatedString and _expression paths. Port-task B001.
B005: Escape rendering trailing space -- hex escape needs trailing space when followed by hex char. ~2 cases. Port-task B005.
B006: list-separator() returns wrong value for empty lists. ~2 cases. Port-task B006.
B007: Quote-flip -- quoted strings with both quote types should flip. ~2 cases. Port-task B007.
B012: Extend namespace unification -- namespace-qualified selectors (*|a, ns|a) fail @extend. ~25 cases. Port-task B012.
B013: Extend superselector trimming -- too many permutations with +/>/~ combinators. ~23 cases. Port-task B013.

— Fixed in commit d9909d2 (+43 cases, 8 validation regressions accepted). url() syntax and modifier parsing added. REOPEN: prior fix was an ad-hoc patch. Requires full faithful port of the original dart-sass method(s).
B016: CSS math function validation -- mod/atan2/exp/sqrt/pow/log etc. do not validate argument count, types, or unit compatibility. ~45 cases. Port-task B016.
B018: Bogus combinator suppression -- leading/trailing combinators (> a, + ~ a) should mark selector as bogus and suppress output. ~23 cases. Port-task B018.
B020: Parent selector & inside :is()/:where()/:matches() at top level should be resolved, not emitted literally. ~10 cases. Port-task B020.
B021: Placeholder selectors (%foo) inside :is()/:where()/:not() should cause argument/rule removal. ~10 cases. Port-task B021.
— Fixed in commit 3a9b196 (+17 cases). User-defined functions now shadow CSS math. REOPEN: prior fix was an ad-hoc patch. Requires full faithful port of the original dart-sass method(s).

Declaration value wiring to _rdExpression causes infinite loop. Root cause: _rdExpression with until callback still hangs on some inputs. Stall detectors (position-based, recursion depth, total iteration count) do not fire — the loop advances the scanner each iteration. Likely the scanner runs past the entire file without hitting a stop condition. Needs: single-test reproduction to identify the exact input, then fix the stop condition or the expression form that eats past boundaries.

isUnique @nowarn annotation (line 431) has inaccurate comment: says 'will be used by _selectorPseudoIsSuperselector in Phase 2' but _isUnique is actually used by _mustUnify (Dart lines 589-597), not _selectorPseudoIsSuperselector. Update the comment to reference _mustUnify as the future caller.

Stale header comment on line 15 says 'isSuperselector delegates to extend functions (stub for now)' but the delegation is now fully implemented via private complexIsSuperselector -> ExtendFunctions.complexIsSuperselector. Remove '(stub for now)' phrasing. Also line 71 says 'which will be ported separately' — it has already been ported. Update to reflect current state.

unifyComplex missing isUseless check: Dart functions.dart line 45 checks 'if (complex.isUseless) return null' inside the for-loop over complexes. Scala line 103 checks 'if (complex.components.isEmpty)' which is a different and narrower check. isUseless (Selector.scala line 58) checks for bogus selectors that can't be transformed into valid CSS by extend/nesting. The components.isEmpty check only catches empty-component selectors. Port the isUseless check faithfully.

unifyComplex extra leading-combinator guard at line 110-113: Scala has 'else if (complex.leadingCombinators.nonEmpty) break(Nullable.empty)' which does not exist in Dart. Dart lines 47-57 use pattern matching: if the complex does NOT match the single-component+single-leading-combinator pattern, it simply falls through to the trailing-combinator logic. The Scala extra guard rejects any complex with >0 leading combinators that doesn't match the single-component pattern, which is stricter than Dart. Remove the else-if branch to match Dart behavior.

unifyComplex extra trailing-combinator guard at lines 121-123: Scala has 'else if (base.combinators.length > 1) break(Nullable.empty)' which does not exist in Dart. Dart lines 60-69 use pattern matching: if the base component does NOT have exactly 1 combinator, the pattern falls through without returning null. The Scala extra guard rejects any base with >1 combinators, which is stricter than Dart. Remove the else-if branch to match Dart behavior.

CompoundSelector.selectorPseudoIsSuperselector :is/:where/:any/:matches path uses approximation instead of full complexIsSuperselector: Line 230 calls private 'complexIsSuperselectorComponents' which is explicitly a 'cheap approximation' (lines 310-322) doing prefix-matching without combinator awareness. Dart functions.dart line 990 calls the full top-level 'complexIsSuperselector' which is the combinator-aware sliding-window algorithm. Now that ExtendFunctions.complexIsSuperselector is fully ported, line 230 should call ExtendFunctions.complexIsSuperselector(complex1.components, target) instead of the private approximation.

unifyComplex docstring at lines 84-88 says 'while skipping the trailing-combinator merging matrix and second-law specificity trimming that drive only rare edge cases'. The trailing-combinator merging IS now ported via mergeTrailingCombinators (lines 368-496). The docstring is stale and should be updated to reflect that the full algorithm is now implemented. Only the second-law specificity trimming remains external (in ExtensionStore).

weave method has two separate docstring blocks at lines 168-176 and 177-178 that should be merged into a single Scaladoc comment. The forceLineBreak description at line 177 is a separate doc comment instead of being part of the main doc comment.
chunks method (line 337) is public ('def chunks[T]') but Dart _chunks (line 618) is private. Should be 'private def chunks[T]' to match Dart visibility.

Stale comment on line 316 says 'which will be ported separately' for listIsSuperselector, but it has already been ported in ExtendFunctions.listIsSuperselector (line 673). Update comment to reflect current state.

unifyComplex missing isUseless check: Dart functions.dart:45 checks 'if (complex.isUseless) return null' at the top of the loop over complexes. Scala line 103 only checks 'complex.components.isEmpty' which is a much narrower test. isUseless returns true when leadingCombinators.length > 1, or any component has combinators.length > 1, or any component's compound contains a bogus pseudo-element. The Scala version will proceed with selectors that the Dart would reject. Fix: replace 'if (complex.components.isEmpty) break(Nullable.empty)' with 'if (complex.isUseless) break(Nullable.empty)' at line 103.

unifyComplex over-strict leading combinator rejection: Dart functions.dart:47-57 uses a pattern match that only captures the (components: [_], leadingCombinators: [var newLeadingCombinator]) case; complexes with leadingCombinators.nonEmpty but components.length > 1 simply fall through and their leadingCombinators are preserved in withoutBases. Scala lines 110-113 break(Nullable.empty) for any complex with nonEmpty leadingCombinators that does not match the single-component/single-leading pattern. This is strictly more restrictive than the Dart. In practice this is largely mitigated because the missing isUseless check (separate issue) would catch many of these cases first. Fix: remove the else-if on lines 110-113 to match Dart fall-through behavior.

selectorPseudoIsSuperselector :is/:matches/:where path uses approximate complexIsSuperselectorComponents instead of full ExtendFunctions.complexIsSuperselector: Dart functions.dart:988-994 calls the real complexIsSuperselector(complex1.components, [...?parents, ComplexSelectorComponent(compound2, const [], compound2.span)]). Scala CompoundSelector.scala:224-231 calls a private complexIsSuperselectorComponents (lines 324-350) that is a simplified greedy-match algorithm ignoring combinators entirely. This means :is() superselector checks involving child/sibling combinators (e.g. ':is(.a > .b)' vs '.a > .b .c') may produce incorrect results. Fix: replace complexIsSuperselectorComponents call with ExtendFunctions.complexIsSuperselector(complex1.components, target).

unifyComplex docstring says 'skipping the trailing-combinator merging matrix': The docstring on Scala lines 86-87 says this is a 'pragmatic port' that skips trailing-combinator merging. However, the actual implementation on lines 116-123 DOES handle trailing combinators (single-combinator merge and multi-combinator rejection). The docstring is stale and misleading. Fix: update the docstring to remove the 'pragmatic port' / 'skipping' language since the implementation handles trailing combinators faithfully.

InterpolationMethod.fromValue factory method not ported - Dart lines 39-82 parse a SassScript value as an interpolation method (handles space name + optional hue method + 'hue' keyword). Required for color.mix() with method argument

_parseChannels not ported - Dart lines 1666-1785 parse color components from space-separated list with slash alpha syntax. Required for modern CSS color syntax like 'lab(50% 20 -30 / 0.5)'. Currently only comma syntax supported.
_parseSlashChannels not ported - Dart lines 1797-1828 parse slash-separated channels for modern CSS color syntax. Required by _parseChannels.
_parseNumberOrString not ported - Dart lines 1831-1837 parse text as number or unquoted string. Used by _parseSlashChannels for parsing alpha after slash.
changeRgb, changeHsl, changeHwb deprecated methods not ported - Dart lines 812-863. These are deprecated but dart-sass still supports them for backwards compatibility.

_removedColorFunction helper not ported - Dart lines 1343-1357. Returns a BuiltInCallable that throws an error directing users to use color.adjust() instead. Used in sass:color module for deprecated functions.
_suggestScaleAndAdjust not ported - Dart lines 1992-2048. Returns suggested translations for deprecated color modification functions. Used in deprecation warnings.
_channelName not ported - Dart lines 2050-2053. Extracts and validates channel name from argument. Simple utility but missing.
_microsoftFilterStart regex not ported - Dart line 23. Used for detecting proprietary Microsoft alpha() filter syntax in global alpha function.

Color functions do not check isLegacy and throw errors for non-legacy colors. Dart-sass lighten(), darken(), saturate(), desaturate(), adjust-hue(), opacify(), transparentize() all throw 'only supported for legacy colors' when passed non-legacy. Port allows non-legacy to pass through silently.
grayscale() for non-legacy colors should use oklch space (set chroma to 0) per Dart lines 974-981. Port only handles legacy HSL path.
mix() function missing isLegacy check and error for mixing non-legacy colors without explicit method. Dart lines 793-807 throw 'To use color.mix() with non-legacy color, you must provide a $method'.
Deprecation warnings for legacy color functions do not include specific suggestions like Dart's _suggestScaleAndAdjust. Port uses generic messages without scale/adjust alternatives.

ISS-255 is invalid: unifyComplex DOES have isUseless check at line 102. Issue description claimed the port checks 'components.isEmpty' instead, but inspection shows 'if (complex.isUseless) break(Nullable.empty)' matching dart-sass line 45. Close this issue.

ISS-036 should be closed: mergeTrailingCombinators (lines 362-490) faithfully ports dart-sass _mergeTrailingCombinators (lines 438-577). All combinator-pair cases are covered: FollowingSibling x FollowingSibling, FollowingSibling x NextSibling (symmetric), Child x NextSibling/FollowingSibling (symmetric), equal combinators, one-side-only. The second-law merge matrix is complete.

ISS-037 should be closed: weave/weaveParents (lines 173-322) faithfully ports dart-sass weave/_weaveParents (lines 231-385). The LCS-based interleaving algorithm is complete, including rootish selector handling, groupSelectors, chunks, and the full mergeTrailingCombinators case table.

ISS-038 should be closed: MergedExtension (lines 118-176) faithfully ports dart-sass merged_extension.dart (79 LOC). The merge() method and unmerge() iterator are complete, including media-context conflict detection and optional-extension optimization.

ISS-039 misattributed: bogus-combinator deprecation warnings are emitted in EvaluateVisitor._warnForBogusCombinators (dart-sass evaluate.dart lines 2485-2530), not in ExtensionStore. Move this issue to EvaluateVisitor.scala if the deprecation warning is desired.

ISS-256 is invalid: the claimed 'extra leading-combinator guard at lines 110-113' does not exist in the current code. Lines 104-109 exactly match dart-sass pattern: only single-component+single-leading-combinator complexes trigger the leading-combinator merge. Close this issue.
InterpolationMethod.fromValue not ported (Dart lines 39-82). Blocks parsing method argument in color.mix().
_parseChannels not ported (Dart lines 1666-1785, 120 LOC). Parses color components from space-separated list with slash alpha. Blocks modern CSS color syntax.
isLegacy validation missing in legacy color functions: lighten/darken/saturate/desaturate/adjust-hue/opacify/transparentize should throw for non-legacy inputs.
grayscale() non-legacy path missing: for non-legacy colors, should convert to oklch and set chroma to 0 instead of using HSL saturation.
colorFromChannelsModern LCH/OKLCH uses channel0 for hue but should use channel2 - line 418 passes channel0 to angleValue for hue but LCH/OKLCH channel order is [0]=lightness [1]=chroma [2]=hue
colorFromChannelsModern HWB missing whiteness+blackness normalization - original lines 1877-1882 normalize when w+b>100 but ported version at lines 387-393 does not

Port line 940-942 uses simple s"$left ${operator.operator} $right". Update once serializer is fully ported.
calc-size() not wired in EvaluateVisitor: need to add calc-size to CSS function dispatch and call SassCalculation.calcSize()

unify() for host/host-context calls super.unify() instead of falling through to the main pseudo-element ordering logic. Original dart-sass pseudo.dart:164-200 does NOT call super when the validation passes — it falls through to the same loop that handles pseudo-element ordering. Port must remove super.unify() call and let both branches share the same loop logic.

unify() line 133: 'if (isElement) Nullable.Null' inside for-loop does not return from the method. In original dart-sass pseudo.dart:188 'if (isElement) return null' returns immediately. Port must use boundary/break to return Nullable.Null when isElement is true inside the loop.
